en: TO THE CHOIRMASTER: ACCORDING TO LILIES. OF DAVID. Save me, O God! For the waters have come up to my neck.
zh: 神啊！求你拯救我， 因為大水淹沒了我。

en: I sink in deep mire, where there is no foothold; I have come into deep waters, and the flood sweeps over me.
zh: 我深陷在淤泥中， 沒有立足之地； 我到了水深之處， 波濤漫過我身。

en: I am weary with my crying out; my throat is parched. My eyes grow dim with waiting for my God.
zh: 我因不住呼求而疲倦，我的喉嚨發乾； 我因等候我的 神，眼睛昏花。

en: More in number than the hairs of my head are those who hate me without cause; mighty are those who would destroy me, those who attack me with lies. What I did not steal must I now restore?
zh: 那些無故恨我的，比我的頭髮還多； 無理與我為敵，要把我滅絕的，人數眾多。 我沒有搶奪過的，竟要我償還。

en: O God, you know my folly; the wrongs I have done are not hidden from you.
zh: 神啊！我的愚昧你是知道的， 我的罪愆不能向你隱瞞。

en: Let not those who hope in you be put to shame through me, O Lord GOD of hosts; let not those who seek you be brought to dishonor through me, O God of Israel.
zh: 主萬軍之耶和華啊！ 願那些等候你的，不要因我蒙羞； 以色列的 神啊！ 願那些尋求你的，不要因我受辱。

en: For it is for your sake that I have borne reproach, that dishonor has covered my face.
zh: 然而，為了你的緣故，我忍受辱罵， 滿面羞愧。

en: I have become a stranger to my brothers, an alien to my mother's sons.
zh: 我的兄弟都疏遠我， 我同母的兄弟把我當作外人。

en: For zeal for your house has consumed me, and the reproaches of those who reproach you have fallen on me.
zh: 因我為你的殿，心中迫切如同火燒； 辱罵你的人的辱罵，都落在我身上。

en: When I wept and humbled my soul with fasting, it became my reproach.
zh: 我哭泣禁食， 這竟成了我的羞辱。

en: When I made sackcloth my clothing, I became a byword to them.
zh: 我披上麻衣， 就成了他們取笑的對象。

en: I am the talk of those who sit in the gate, and the drunkards make songs about me.
zh: 坐在城門口的人對我議論紛紛， 我成了酒徒之歌。

en: But as for me, my prayer is to you, O LORD. At an acceptable time, O God, in the abundance of your steadfast love answer me in your saving faithfulness.
zh: 但是，耶和華啊！ 在悅納的時候，我向你禱告； 神啊！求你按著你豐盛的慈愛， 憑著你信實的拯救應允我。

en: Deliver me from sinking in the mire; let me be delivered from my enemies and from the deep waters.
zh: 求你救我脫離淤泥， 不要容我沉下去； 求你救我脫離那些恨我的人， 救我脫離深水。

en: Let not the flood sweep over me, or the deep swallow me up, or the pit close its mouth over me.
zh: 求你不要讓波濤淹沒我， 不要讓深水吞滅我， 也不要讓深坑把我封閉。

en: Answer me, O LORD, for your steadfast love is good; according to your abundant mercy, turn to me.
zh: 耶和華啊！求你應允我，因為你的慈愛美善； 求你照著你豐盛的憐憫轉臉垂顧我。

en: Hide not your face from your servant; for I am in distress; make haste to answer me.
zh: 求你不要向你的僕人掩面； 求你快快應允我，因為我在困境之中。

en: Draw near to my soul, redeem me; ransom me because of my enemies!
zh: 求你親近我，拯救我， 因我仇敵的緣故救贖我。

en: You know my reproach, and my shame and my dishonor; my foes are all known to you.
zh: 我所受的辱罵、欺凌和侮辱，你都知道， 我所有的敵人都在你面前。

en: Reproaches have broken my heart, so that I am in despair. I looked for pity, but there was none, and for comforters, but I found none.
zh: 辱罵傷了我的心， 我憂愁難過； 我希望有人同情，卻一個也沒有； 我希望有人安慰，還是找不到一個。

en: They gave me poison for food, and for my thirst they gave me sour wine to drink.
zh: 他們在我的食物中加上苦膽， 我渴了，他們把醋給我喝。

en: Let their own table before them become a snare; and when they are at peace, let it become a trap.
zh: 願他們的筵席在他們面前變為網羅、 報應和陷阱 。

en: Let their eyes be darkened, so that they cannot see, and make their loins tremble continually.
zh: 願他們的眼睛昏花，不能看見； 願他們的腰不停地戰抖。

en: Pour out your indignation upon them, and let your burning anger overtake them.
zh: 求你把你的惱怒傾倒在他們身上， 使你的烈怒追上他們。

en: May their camp be a desolation; let no one dwell in their tents.
zh: 願他們的住處變為荒場； 願他們的帳幕無人居住。

en: For they persecute him whom you have struck down, and they recount the pain of those you have wounded.
zh: 因為他們迫害你所擊打的人， 嘲笑你所擊傷的人的痛苦。

en: Add to them punishment upon punishment; may they have no acquittal from you.
zh: 願你在他們的懲罰上加上懲罰， 不容他們在你面前得稱為義。

en: Let them be blotted out of the book of the living; let them not be enrolled among the righteous.
zh: 願他們的名字從生命冊上被塗抹， 不要讓他們和義人一同被記錄。

en: But I am afflicted and in pain; let your salvation, O God, set me on high!
zh: 至於我，我是憂傷痛苦的人； 神啊！願你的救恩保護我。

en: I will praise the name of God with a song; I will magnify him with thanksgiving.
zh: 我要用詩歌讚美 神的名， 以感謝尊他為大。

en: This will please the LORD more than an ox or a bull with horns and hoofs.
zh: 這就使耶和華喜悅，勝過獻牛， 或是獻有角有蹄的公牛。

en: When the humble see it they will be glad; you who seek God, let your hearts revive.
zh: 困苦的人看見了就喜樂； 尋求 神的人哪！願你們的心甦醒。

en: For the LORD hears the needy and does not despise his own people who are prisoners.
zh: 因為耶和華垂聽了貧窮人的禱告， 也不藐視屬他自己、正被囚禁的人。

en: Let heaven and earth praise him, the seas and everything that moves in them.
zh: 願天和地都讚美他， 願海洋和海中一切生物都讚美他。

en: For God will save Zion and build up the cities of Judah, and people shall dwell there and possess it;
zh: 因為 神要拯救錫安， 要建造猶大的眾城； 他的子民必在那裡居住，並且擁有那地為業。

en: the offspring of his servants shall inherit it, and those who love his name shall dwell in it.
zh: 他眾僕人的後裔都必承受那地為業， 喜愛他名的人要住在其中。 大衛的記念詩，交給詩班長。
